rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical carboxylative cyclization of 2-isocyano-styrenes with CO2 using a sacrificial Mg anode to give 2-oxindole-3-acetic acids.
electrochemical_role: Cathodic reduction generates a CO2 radical anion (and/or substrate-derived radical) while the Mg anode is sacrificially oxidized to Mg(II); the CO2 radical anion adds to the vinyl isocyanide, triggering radical cyclization onto the isocyano carbon.

key_intermediates.name: alpha-carboxyl benzyl radical from CO2 addition to vinyl group
imidoyl radical from cyclization onto isocyanide carbon
imidoyl anion / Mg-amide after further reduction

electron_flow: At the Fe cathode, CO2 is reduced to CO2 radical anion which adds to the terminal alkene of the 2-isocyanostyrene; the resulting benzylic radical cyclizes onto the isocyanide to form an imidoyl radical, which is further reduced and protonated/formylated, while the Mg anode is consumed to balance charge.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction completely suppressed; starting material 1a recovered in 86% yield, indicating a radical intermediate is intercepted.

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction partially proceeds, giving the desired acid 2a (22%) and the deformyl/desaturated byproduct 3a (3,5-dimethyl-2-oxo-3-phenylindoline-1-carbaldehyde, 21%), consistent with a radical pathway not fully quenched by BHT.

notes: Only the radical trapping experiments and a cyclic voltammetry section are visible on this page; CV data details and any DFT support are not shown. The formation of byproduct 3a under BHT conditions hints at a competing pathway lacking the CO2-derived carboxymethyl group, suggesting CO2 incorporation occurs after initial radical generation.
